sessions.intent: Design the filesystem permission system: signed per-app grants on paths, with read/write/delete verbs each carrying sub-options. Path-shaped rewrite of IPLangFileSystem.

before.assumptions: Current FileAccessControl is runtime-only (in-memory FileAccessControl record, populated by yes/no/always prompt at exception time). No signature, no expiry, no audit. IPLangFileSystem inherits System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem (string-only). Cross-app reads (e.g. a messages app reading every app's system.sqlite) are not modelable today.
before.risk: Big surface rewrite — every action that touches the FS and every runtime internal that does the same. Signature work has to walk the full call graph. Goal-mapped FS provider is deliberately deferred.

actions.context: Added new section 'OBP Variant Design — When a Concept Has Multiple Configurable Modes'. Codifies four rules drawn from this design pass: singular folders, variant records with default-allow, owners-do-their-own-coverage, methods take whole domain objects. Also added a refinement to existing OBP guidance: verb-named methods are fine when they do real work — the GetX/IsX smell is about property-shaped questions, not verbs in general.

v1 plan for filesystem permission system. Spine + four topic deep-dives + five stage files + summary. Design settled in conversation with Ingi; three open conversation gates documented before stage 3.

after.status: plan written; awaiting Ingi to settle the three open gates (app-side cascade, Content sub-option on Read, process-only grant distinction) before stage 3 starts. Stage 1 can begin in parallel — pure types are independent.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end (file.read from Messages to Email/system.sqlite), every concept has a named owner, no choreography across files.
after.notes: Branch newly created from origin/runtime2. good_to_know.md edit is real codification work, not session noise — captures lessons that should outlast this branch.

sessions.intent: Rewrite the plan to reflect a major design shift: permission generalizes per-subsystem (file/http/payment), Data.Request becomes a third terminal state, permission lives on the variable via Properties (lazy from store), and stages 1-3 bundle types + storage + full IPLangFileSystem refactor with permission baked in.

before.assumptions: Original plan v1 framed PermissionRequired as an Error type, kept the FS surface rewrite separate from the permission check site, and treated this branch as filesystem-only with no generalization story. Conversation with Ingi reframed all three.
before.risk: The shift is large enough that a partial update would leave the plan internally inconsistent. Stage files and topic docs need to move together with the spine.

actions.context: Rewrote spine + topic docs. Added runtime-flow.md (new). plan.md now leads with the pattern-not-base-class generalization, Data.Request third state, and the (c) bundle in stage 4. permission-design.md drops Check/Describe methods on the record — it's pure data with Covers. storage.md describes the two-store unification (in-memory + sqlite via system variables). filesystem-surface.md becomes the bundled IPLangFileSystem v2 design with permission baked in. open-questions.md retires most of the original list; ships five smaller still-open items.

Renumbered stages. Old stage-2 (storage), stage-3 (FS surface), stage-4 (PermissionRequired error) deleted; new stage-2 (Data.Request state), stage-3 (storage), stage-4 (IPLangFileSystem v2 bundle) written. Stage 1 (types) and stage 5 (e2e) updated in place. New ordering reflects the dependency chain: types → engine state → storage → FS rewrite → e2e.
Prepended 2026-05-19 entry summarizing the 10 design shifts and what previously-open questions resolved. Kept the 2026-05-14 entry below for history.

after.status: Plan rewritten initially to reflect 2026-05-19 design pass. Five stages defined, all pending. Stage 1 can start as-is (pure types, no dependencies). The big bundle is stage 4 — needs stages 2 and 3 first.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end through file.read. Generalization pattern named for HTTP/Payment without prematurely codifying.
after.notes: Two follow-up branches implied by this design: an http-permission branch and a payment-permission branch, both consuming the Ask infrastructure landed in stage 2 of this branch.

sessions.intent: Walk through the plan with Ingi section by section; refine the design as it surfaces issues; propagate corrections across all plan files in one consolidated pass.

before.assumptions: Initial rewrite at 2026-05-19T13:00 had Data.Request as a third terminal state, FilePermission with AppId/Path/Match/Verb, storage as 'system variables backed by sqlite,' permission/@this as a manager class, and one-line code snippets in stage files.
before.risk: Walking the plan in interactive mode tends to surface design problems mid-step; without consolidating fixes, the plan grows inconsistent. Several corrections from Ingi struck OBP nerves: helper-soup, envelope-thinking, yes-man drift.

actions.context: Spine: section 1 framing rewritten to drop Data.Request as a third state in favour of Data.Fail with an Ask marker handled inside error.handle's built-in path. Section 3 runtime narrative rewritten — Path delegates to actor.Permission (not App.Permissions); engine owns retry loop; ask handler is stateless. Cross-cutting decisions updated for 2-column storage rule, actor-as-record-field, JSON-filter scoping. Stage 2 renamed/relinked to stage-2-ask-routing.md.

Topic docs rewritten. runtime-flow.md: ask handling inside error.handle, two-layer lookup (Properties cache → actor.Permission), engine-side retry. permission-design.md: FilePermission gains Actor field; Path.CheckPermission takes verb kind only; no ReadOnly/WriteOnly factories; the manager-class concept dissolved into actor.Permission. storage.md: rewritten around App.SettingsStore + 2-column rule + actor-as-JSON-filter; no separate persistence layer; one permission table not three. filesystem-surface.md: code blocks stripped (architect rule); permission check delegated to Path via path.CheckPermission. open-questions.md: throw-vs-return resolved as return; ask shape resolved as Data.Fail+Ask.

Stage files updated consistently. Stage 1 references Ask marker work as stage 2's concern. Stage 2 renamed (data-request-state → ask-routing), reframed around error.handle's built-in path. Stage 3 reframed around Actor.@this.Permission (per-actor view) + path.CheckPermission on Path class; storage is App.SettingsStore 'permission' table with 2-column rule; per-actor scoping via JSON filter on Actor field. Stage 4 references path.CheckPermission(Verb.X) interface. Stage 5 acceptance checks the permission table directly.

New file. Proposes a 5th OBP smell to good_to_know.md: 'Helper that takes a domain object and returns a derived answer.' Includes worked example contrasting helper-soup (Resolve(path), CheckPermission(absolute, verb)) with self-owning methods (path.Absolute, path.CheckPermission(verb)).

Prepended a new 2026-05-19 (continued) entry summarizing the refinements: storage on App.SettingsStore directly; 2-column rule; actor-on-record with JSON-filter scoping; path.CheckPermission(verb kind) signature; verb sub-options stay in C#; four-level delegation; error.handle is the umbrella; CLAUDE.md proposal filed.

after.status: Plan internally consistent end-to-end after the walkthrough. All stage files, topic docs, summary, and the CLAUDE.md proposal reflect the same model. Stage 1 can begin; stage 2 has a clear acceptance contract; stage 3 has a concrete shape for actor.Permission + Path.CheckPermission; stage 4 references both.
after.health: good — design walked end-to-end with Ingi, multiple OBP corrections caught and incorporated. The plan now reads as one coherent design rather than a sequence of accumulated decisions.
after.notes: Major patterns codified during the session: (1) FilePermission carries Actor field; (2) Path owns CheckPermission; (3) actor.Permission unifies in-memory + persisted; (4) error.handle owns ask routing internally; (5) 2-column storage rule. The helper-soup OBP smell was the most generalizable lesson — filed as a CLAUDE.md proposal.

sessions.intent: Translate architect's 5-stage filesystem-permission design into a test contract — C# TUnit stubs + PLang test goals — that the coder makes pass.

before.assumptions: Architect plan is final after Ingi's review pass (33 comments resolved). 5 stages: types, snapshot/resume, Path.Authorize, storage, IPLangFileSystem v2, end-to-end Messages. Existing CallbackTests/ folder will be rewritten around Snapshot instead of per-callback classes.
before.risk: Volume is large (~100 tests). Stage 4 parametrization needs the coder to confirm the [Arguments] shape. 'No App.Run / App.RunAction' tests are static-survey style — that pattern may not be established yet.

actions.context: 10-batch breakdown across the 5 stages. Approved up front by user — no per-batch walk-through needed.
Stage 1 — Verb sub-record cover matrix + Permission.Covers with closed Match dispatch and JSON round-trip.
Stage 2a — markers, Snapshot field+helper, ShouldExit, RunFrom, RunAsync, output.ask routing, Snapshot.Resume. One concern per file.
Stage 2a — PLang integration. Mid-goal stateful + cross-goal stateless suspend/resume (the canonical Start→AskAQuestion case).
Stage 2b — Path.Authorize answer cases (a/y/n/garbage), stateless bubble, constructed Permission shape, PermissionDenied; plus stateful and stateless e2e.
Stage 3 — Find/Add/Revoke; per-actor JSON-filter scoping; in-memory + sqlite unification; signature caching; idempotent Add.

Stage 4 — parametrized per-method flow (in-root, out-of-root stateful, out-of-root stateless); shape contracts (no IFileSystem inheritance, no ValidatePath, no FileAccessControl); Move/Copy bundled consent. Parametrization shape (MethodDataSource) will need a coder review.

Stage 5 — six-step integration test broken into six independent PLang test goals: no-grant suspend → grant 'a' stores → immediate reread skips → restart still skips → revoke reprompts → narrowed grant rejects wider request.

after.status: Test contract drafted. ~70 C# test stubs + 11 PLang test goal stubs. All bodies are Assert.Fail / throw "not implemented". Ready for coder.
after.notes: Open follow-ups (logged in plan.md): (1) some Batch 6 'stateful e2e' tests may actually need real Stage 3 storage rather than mocks — coder to decide; (2) TUnit MethodDataSource shape in Stage 4 parametrization needs a coder review; (3) static-survey 'no App.Run / App.RunAction' tests may need a new helper.

sessions.intent: Codeanalyzer review of filesystem-permission v1 (stages 1-5): OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, deletion test on ~6k LOC of new/modified C#.

before.assumptions: Coder reports 2830/2830 tests passing. Five stages landed: Permission types, snapshot-resume engine, Path.Authorize, Actor.Permission storage, IPLangFileSystem v2 surface. Several items deferred (v1 surface deletion, scenario 4 deserialiser bug, .test.goal integration, App.RunAction surface, Cause linkage, wire serialiser).
before.risk: Big surface — Snapshot-resume crosses Engine/Goal/Step/Action/Channel boundaries; permission state lives in Actor; FS v2 surface added alongside v1 (dual-path risk). Look for OBP shape smells from doubled collections, cross-file locks, and disciplined collections held in the wrong type.

actions.context: v1 plan — five passes (OBP rules+shape, simplification, readability, behavioral, deletion) against the 43 modified C# files under PLang/App/.

Pass 1a/1b: OBP shape clean across the family. Pass 2: 9x authorize preamble copy-paste in Path.Operations; Stat returns Dictionary; AlwaysExpiry constant unused; await Task.FromResult redundant. Pass 3: prefix-concatenation in Authorize, Channel/Stream leaveOpen comment, Step break condition deserves a one-line comment or named predicate. Pass 4: sync-over-async at 4 sites in Actor.Permission; unbounded recursion in Path.Authorize and BundledTransfer on bad input; silent bare catch in VerifySignature; IsInRoot uses OrdinalIgnoreCase on a case-sensitive OS; exception->ServiceError pattern duplicated between Step.RunAsync and Step.RunFrom. Pass 5: AlwaysExpiry constant, Cause chain (Call._ownCause always null, renderer Cause branches dead), cause/erroredCall parameters threaded but unused in error/handle's RunRecovery, await Task.FromResult wrapper.

Findings written up with file:line citations and reproduction-ready fixes. Verdict: NEEDS WORK (fail). Top issues: sync-over-async at Actor.Permission, unbounded recursion in Authorize/BundledTransfer, silent VerifySignature catch, case-insensitive IsInRoot on Linux, 9x authorize preamble, dead Cause/cause parameters after stage 2a.5.

after.status: verdict written — NEEDS WORK. 10 findings cited with file:line. Shape (Pass 1a/1b) clean; behavioral and copy-paste issues. Coder follow-up suggested in summary.md.
after.health: good — the OBP shape of the new code is right, which is the big risk on a 6k-LOC engine restructure. The findings are tactical, not structural.
after.notes: Worth noting: report.json had no coder session block even though the coder committed 14 stage commits and wrote a long summary.md. Not a bug per se — but if reporting is meant to be complete, the coder's session is missing from the audit trail.

sessions.intent: Close codeanalyzer v1's remaining doc-comment debt for findings #7 and #8 (dead Cause chain). The earlier 1af7922 commit removed the Cause code, but five stale doc-comments survived — including a broken <see cref="Cause"/>.

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v1 cited 10 findings. By 13:05 UTC, all code-level fixes had landed (af32f3e, 82a136b, c4cbbd3, 8b22a5e, f543e19, 1af7922). A grep for 'Cause' across PLang/App/ still surfaced five XML doc-comments, one of which (CallStack/Call/this.cs:13) used <see cref="Cause"/> against a property that no longer exists.
before.risk: Low. Comment-only edits; one broken cref to remove.

actions.context: Removed 'Cause' from four structural-data comments and the broken <see cref="Cause"/> in CallStack/Call/this.cs. Trimmed the stale 'recovery boundaries with their originating Cause' note in Error.cs. +4/-8 lines across 5 files.
v2 coder report documenting the post-codeanalyzer follow-ups, a verdict.json marking pass, and a summary.md preamble pointing readers at v2.

after.status: All 10 codeanalyzer v1 findings closed. Commit 91a7999. dotnet build clean. 2846/2846 C# tests green.
after.health: good — codeanalyzer v1 verdict (NEEDS WORK) is now fully resolved across code and comments.
after.notes: Earlier coder commits had no report.json session entry (codeanalyzer flagged this). This v2 entry covers only the comment cleanup; the eight earlier fix commits remain undocumented in report.json.

sessions.intent: Close branch: fix codeanalyzer v2 #2 (ValidatePath case-comparison regression), defer v2 #1 (handler-layer authorize copy-paste) to a new branch with a written architect handoff plan. The plan goes further than codeanalyzer's (a)/(b) options — Path becomes polymorphic across schemes (file:// / http:// / s3://...).

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v2 verdict NEEDS WORK. Two regressions cited: v2 #1 (handler-layer authorize copy-paste in modules/file/*.cs) and v2 #2 (PLangFileSystem.ValidatePath:227 Linux case-comparison). Ingi's call after design discussion: close this branch with v2 #2 fixed and v2 #1 deferred to a polymorphic-Path branch.
before.risk: Low. v2 #2 is a single-line site fix behind a shared helper. v2 #1's deferral is documented in three places (path-polymorphism-plan.md, todos.md, coder summary v3) so codeanalyzer's next pass sees the intentional scope choice.

sessions.plan: Documentation/v0.2/path-polymorphism-plan.md (for v2 #1, future branch) + .bot/filesystem-permission/coder/v3/report.md (for v2 #2 + branch closure)

actions.context: Hoisted Path.RootComparison static helper into Path.cs main partial. Path.Authorize.IsInRoot now reads the shared helper instead of inlining the OS switch. PLangFileSystem.ValidatePath:227 changed from OrdinalIgnoreCase to Path.RootComparison. Single home for the rule, no future drift.

Architect handoff plan for polymorphic Path (Phase 1: FilePath split + factory + handler degenerates; Phase 2: scheme registry + HttpPath; Phase 3: community schemes). Closes codeanalyzer v2 #1 on a new branch — deliberately not this one. Includes verb-surface matrix, scheme-registry [PathScheme] attribute design, open architect questions (factory location, caching, PlangType split). Credentials scoped down (PLang identity for HTTP, Settings for custom — base class doesn't abstract over creds). todos.md updated with one-line pointer.
v3 coder report explicitly tells codeanalyzer that v2 #1 is tracked-with-plan (not punted to vague follow-up). Verdict: ready-to-merge.

after.status: Branch closes. v2 #2 fixed. v2 #1 has a written architect handoff plan. dotnet build clean; 2846/2846 C# tests green.
after.health: good — every codeanalyzer finding from v1 and v2 is either fixed or deferred with a written, phased plan that codeanalyzer can track on the next branch.
after.notes: Communication to codeanalyzer about v2 #1's deferral is in three places by design: the plan doc (the artifact), todos.md (the durable index), and the coder summary v3 (the branch-local audit trail). Codeanalyzer's next pass on this branch should see ready-to-merge.

sessions.intent: Validate test quality for coder v3 filesystem-permission work — hunt false greens.

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v3 PASS; coder declared branch ready-to-merge; 2846/2846 C# green per coder.
before.risk: v3's single behavioral change (ValidatePath RootComparison) is review-driven code — highest risk for missing test coverage.

actions.context: Clean rebuild + ran both suites. C# 2846/2846 pass; PLang 213 pass / 6 fail. Verified the 6 PLang fails are intentional fail-fixtures (_fixtures_fail/_fixtures_sensitive) by reading fixture content + git provenance — not coder regressions.

Deletion test on v3's only behavioral change (ValidatePath:227 OrdinalIgnoreCase->RootComparison): reverting it fails 0 tests. No test supplies a case-variant-of-root path. 100% line/branch coverage = coverage dazzle. Major false-green, finding 1.
Parameter-swap test on PerformTransfer isMove flag: no test verifies Move removes the source. Flipping isMove keeps the suite green. Major weak-assertion, finding 2.

Read all 8 permission .test.goal files + verified GrantAStoresPersisted .pr. 6 of 8 are in-root round-trips named after permission scenarios they never exercise — plang --test reports them green under scenario names. Major false-green, finding 3.
Wrote test-report.json (9 findings, verdict needs-fixes), result.md, verdict.json, coverage.json, summary.md.

after.status: NEEDS-FIXES. 3 major false-greens + 5 minor + 1 process violation. All are test-quality gaps; no code bug — codeanalyzer v3 already passed the code and the v3 fix is correct.
after.health: Suite green (2846/2846 C#) but dishonest: v3's case-comparison fix, Move semantics, and 6 PLang permission goals are false greens.
after.notes: No baseline-tests.md in any coder version (finding 9) — regression attribution reconstructed manually, confirmed no regressions. Fixes for findings 1-3 are cheap: ~1 ValidatePath test, 1 Move assertion block, rename/delete 6 goals.

sessions.intent: Re-review test quality for coder v4 (closes 9 tester-v3 findings) + v5 (drops PermissionRecord.AppId, un-skips Scenario4).

before.assumptions: coder declared v5 ready; C# 2853 pass / 0 skip / 0 fail per coder. v3 verdict was NEEDS-FIXES with 3 major false-greens.
before.risk: v5 is real production code (AppId removal from a security record), not just test code — highest risk. A fix is only closed if the mutation v3 survived now kills a test.

actions.context: Clean rebuild + both suites. C# 2853/2853 pass, 0 skip; PLang 203/203 pass (4 intentional fail-fixtures excluded, confirmed via coder/baseline-tests.md). No regressions.

Mutation tests (source edited then reverted, never committed): RootComparison->OrdinalIgnoreCase kills IsInRoot_UpperCasedRoot test (F1); isMove->File.Copy kills both Move_* tests (F2); disabling persisted Find kills Scenario4 (F4/v5). All three v3 major false-greens now genuinely gated.

Reviewed v5's PermissionRecord.AppId removal for security regressions. Sound: AppId was a per-instance GUID that never survived a restart — it defeated the persist contract rather than adding isolation. Actor isolation via name filter + signature verify; root isolation via per-root SettingsStore. Scenario4 is the cross-App regression gate.
Wrote v4 review docs (verdict pass) and, on explicit user request after the user was alarmed by mid-review source edits, a CLAUDE.md proposal for an announce-before-mutation rule.

after.status: PASS. All 9 v3 findings closed and mutation-verified. v5 AppId removal reviewed as a correct, well-scoped production change.
after.health: Suite green and now honest — the three major false-greens each die under the mutation v3 survived. Three minor non-blocking notes (N1 docstring over-claim, N2 line-227 defensive, N3 no different-root isolation test).
after.notes: coder v4/v5 did not append sessions to report.json. v5 production code has not had a codeanalyzer pass — worth one before branch close.

sessions.intent: Security audit of the filesystem-permission branch — consent-gated FS access, signed permission grants, the v5 AppId-removal trust-boundary change.

before.assumptions: Branch adds consent-gated filesystem access: file action handlers call Path.Authorize, which prompts the user, signs the grant, stores it via Actor.Permission. v5 dropped PermissionRecord.AppId so persisted grants survive a fresh App on the same root.
before.risk: Dropping AppId widens a signed grant's trust scope. If the per-actor sqlite store is not actually the persistence boundary, an 'always allow' grant could be replayed across Apps. Also: signature gate on grant verification, path-traversal in the root check, RootComparison helper not reaching all gate sites.

actions.context: Mapped the consent gate. Grant creation is C#-internal and consent-gated — no PLang-reachable fabrication path (no permission.add module; settings.set hardcodes its table). Path containment correct (trailing-separator prefix). This half of the gate holds.

Audited grant verification. F1: signing.verify checks signature integrity but not signer authority — any keypair verifies. F2: TryCover auto-trusts unsigned persisted rows. F3: signing.verify's 5-min Created-age freshness window is mis-applied to long-lived persisted grants, silently expiring 'a' grants and false-greening Scenario4. F4: RegexMatches has no match timeout.
Wrote security report (4 findings, attack-surface map), result.md, verdict (PASS — no critical/high open), summary.

after.health: Consent gate structurally sound on the creation side; weaknesses are all on the verification side and either latent (F1/F4) or fail-closed (F3) today.
after.notes: F1 (signer identity not pinned) and F3 (persisted grants expire after 5min; test false-green) should be closed before the permission table gains any non-disk write path. F2/F4 are cheap defense-in-depth. None block merge.

sessions.intent: Cross-cutting integrity audit of filesystem-permission — review gaps between codeanalyzer, tester, security

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v3 PASS, tester v4 PASS, security v1 PASS (4 non-blocking findings). All three reviewed their slice and approved.
before.risk: Branch diverged from runtime2 before the app-lowercase merge — runtime2 now has lowercase app/. Possible review gap: tester says Scenario4 is a real persistence gate, security says it false-greens the 5-min-expiry feature.

actions.context: Read all three prior reviewer reports + architect/coder summaries to map what each slice covered and where the seams are.

Verified F3 chain end-to-end (EnsureSigned no Expires -> VerifySignature no TimeoutMs -> Ed25519 step2 Created-age check = 5min). Confirmed all 7 file handlers route through Authorize. Checked ResumeChain frame disposal ordering. Checked merge-base vs runtime2.
Wrote audit findings: F-A (major, 5-min grant expiry + false-greening test), F-B (major, branch on stale runtime2), F-C/F-D minor, F-E nit. Verdict FAIL.

after.health: Branch builds clean (0 errors), C# suite 2853/2853 green. Internally sound; FAIL is about a documented-contract defect (F-A) and an unreviewed integration gap (F-B), not build/test breakage.
after.notes: F-A: persisted always-allow grants expire after 5 min — security under-rated as non-blocking; escalated to major because the doc-comment is false and Scenario4 false-greens it. F-B: merge-base predates app-lowercase merge; ~40 new PascalCase-namespace files vs runtime2 lowercase convention. Coverage verified independently.

sessions.intent: Review coder v4/v5/v6 (test-quality, AppId drop, SkipFreshnessCheck + runtime2 merge) for OBP, simplicity, behavioral correctness.

before.assumptions: Auditor FAILed v5 with F-A (grant durability) + F-B (merge). Coder v6 claims both closed. v5/v6 are small logic changes; merge is mechanical rename.
before.risk: SkipFreshnessCheck disables anti-replay primitives for grant verification — must skip exactly the right checks and keep wire-message verification intact (default false).

actions.context: v5 AppId drop: verified record ctor 5->4 args, Covers, Find/Revoke/TryCover/BuildRequest all updated in lockstep, no dangling AppId. _inMemory list discipline fully in-class. OBP clean.

v6 SkipFreshnessCheck: traced 5-step VerifyAsync. Flag skips exactly step 2 (wire-freshness) + step 4 (nonce-replay); step 3 (Expires) still always runs. Default-false keeps wire-message verification intact; Permission.VerifySignature is the only caller passing true and the only grant-verify path. ('','true') Data ctor is established idiom (3 precedents).
PASS verdict. Two non-blocking carry-overs: F-C OrdinalIgnoreCase sites (now tracked in coder v6 report); pre-existing Path-only Add keying. Clean rebuild: 0 errors.

after.notes: Coder v5/v6 close auditor F-A and F-B cleanly. No OBP violations, no bugs, build 0 errors. Deferred F-C/D/E + security F1/F2/F4 honestly tracked in coder/v6/report.md.

sessions.intent: v2 security re-audit after runtime2 merge and the SkipFreshnessCheck crypto change.

before.assumptions: v1 PASS with 4 findings (F1/F2 signer-trust gaps, F3 5-min grant expiry, F4 ReDoS). F3 reportedly closed by coder v6.
before.risk: The freshness/nonce-replay bypass added for grants could leak to wire-message verification if mis-scoped.

actions.context: Re-audited the coder v6 SkipFreshnessCheck crypto change. Traced every signing.verify construction site: flag is [Default(false)], only the grant path sets true, all 4 HTTP wire-verify sites leave it false. Ed25519 step 8 (signature verification) still runs for grants. F3 fixed and correctly scoped — no leak to wire-message anti-replay.

v2 security report. F3 closed; F1 (medium)/F2 (low)/F4 (low) carried forward unchanged, all precondition-gated by the no-non-disk-write-path-to-permission-table invariant, re-confirmed at HEAD. Verdict PASS.

after.health: green: C# suite 2855/2855 on clean rebuild; no critical/high open findings
after.notes: F3 fixed by coder v6 and re-audited safe. F1/F2/F4 open but gated. One non-blocking observation logged: callback/channel verify doc-comment contradiction (pre-existing, not a branch finding).

sessions.intent: Re-audit filesystem-permission after auditor v1 FAIL — verify F-A and F-B are genuinely closed.

before.assumptions: codeanalyzer v4, tester v6, security v2 all re-passed after coder v5/v6/v7. Auditor v1 FAILed on F-A (5-min grant expiry) and F-B (stale runtime2 merge-base).
before.risk: A re-reviewer may have rubber-stamped a code change without verifying the headline contract; the F-A doc-comment cleanup may be incomplete.

actions.context: Traced the F-A fix end-to-end: SkipFreshnessCheck skips Ed25519 steps 2+4, step 3 (Expires) and step 8 (crypto) still run for grants, default-false keeps wire path intact. Verified F-B: origin/runtime2 is an ancestor of HEAD, app/ lowercase. Adjudicated F-C: authorization gate uses RootComparison, the OrdinalIgnoreCase sites are observability only.

Auditor v2 PASS. Both v1 majors genuinely closed. One new minor (F-1): stale inverted doc-comment in actor/permission/this.cs class header that the F-A fix should have cleaned up. Three v1 minors carried non-blocking.

after.notes: F-A and F-B closed. F-1 (minor doc inversion) worth fixing before docs propagates it; does not block. Next stage: docs.

sessions.intent: v5 review — verify coder v7 test-only addition closing tester v5 F1 (nonce-replay half of F-A).

before.assumptions: v4 PASSed at coder v6. Only new code since is coder v7 (one regression test). Tester v6, security v2, auditor v2 all PASSed → expect clean.
before.risk: Low — no production-code change; only risk is that the new test is shaped wrong (false-green, brittle, or doesn't actually exercise step 4).

actions.context: Reviewed the +27/-0 addition (Scenario4_PersistedGrantReVerified_NonceReplayDoesNotReprompt) in coder v7. Test pairs with the existing Scenario4_WireFreshnessWindow to give independent coverage of Ed25519 verify steps 2 and 4. Mutation-verified by coder. CLEAN.
v5 PASS artifacts. summary.md overwritten per workflow.

Codeanalyzer learnings from this branch — 7 entries covering auditor v2 F1 (the sibling-class doc-comment miss in actor/permission/this.cs:11-13), the test-only-commit pitfall from tester v5, the SkipFreshnessCheck bypass-scoping template from security v2, idiom-precedent-citation, scenario test naming, and the codeanalyzer-no-CLAUDE.md-proposals rule.

Added auditor v2 F1 self-correction block to report and summary — the inverted doc-comment in actor/permission/this.cs:11-13 that v4 missed and v5's initial pass did not re-check. Verdict stays PASS (doc-only, code is correct) with a 4-line follow-up flagged for the coder.

after.notes: v5 PASS holds. Ingi prompted re-read of auditor v2; captured the sibling-doc-inversion miss as Learning #1 and flagged the trivial follow-up in the report. Branch advances after coder lands the 4-line doc fix.
